verdict: Bookkeeping and routine reconciliation are nearly fully automatable today; full-scope accountants survive because of audit, advisory, and CPA-licensed signoff. The role is bifurcating into 'data entry that's gone' and 'judgment + advisory that grows'.
timeline_callout: ERP + AI close-the-books tools are deployed at major firms now; the squeeze on entry-level accounting headcount is already underway.

routine_cognitive.reasoning: Reconciliation, journal entries, and routine reporting are pattern-matching on structured data — top automation territory.

creative_judgment.reasoning: Audit and tax planning require judgment; basic compliance does not.

regulatory_moat.reasoning: CPAs sign audits and tax returns under personal liability; non-CPA accountants have a thinner moat.

stakes_of_error.reasoning: Material errors trigger restatements and SEC scrutiny — humans stay in the loop on signoff.

relationships.reasoning: Client advisory matters; routine bookkeeping does not.

ai_capability_gap.reasoning: Modern accounting platforms ship with AI assistants for the bulk of recurring work.

key_tasks.note: Most of the heavy lifting is now templated + AI-assisted.

key_tasks.task: Prepare tax returns and tax planning advice
key_tasks.note: Software automates compute; CPA judgment + signoff remain human.
Sampling and exception detection are AI-assisted; audit opinion is human.
McKinsey 2024 — 86% automatable; this is the most exposed sub-task.

evidence_ledger.meta: US Dept. of Labor · Updated Jan 2026
evidence_ledger.contribution: Standardized task list — heavy on structured data manipulation.
Bookkeeping 86% automatable; full accountant role somewhat lower due to advisory tasks.
Accounting clerks listed among top declining roles.
Accountant exposure score is among the higher white-collar values.
Projects 6% growth for accountants — advisory work expanding even as bookkeeping shrinks.

reasoning_steps.title: Score the eight dimensions
reasoning_steps.body: routine_cognitive=8, ai_capability_gap=8, creative_judgment=4, regulatory_moat=6, embodied_work=0, stakes_of_error=5, relationships=4, physical_dexterity=0.

reasoning_steps.title: Calibration check
reasoning_steps.body: Anchor table lists Accountant at 65. Computed 75 is 10 above. Soften toward 65 — the CPA licensure moat and advisory expansion at the senior end justify a lower score than the dimension math alone suggests.

reasoning_steps.title: Confidence band
reasoning_steps.body: High ±7 — McKinsey, Felten, and WEF all converge on high exposure for the routine portion.

adjacent_careers.name: Auditor (CPA)
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Licensure + signoff liability are real moats; the work survives the automation wave.

adjacent_careers.name: Tax Advisor (CPA)
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Same moat plus advisory relationship work.

adjacent_careers.name: Financial Analyst
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Forward-looking analysis vs backward-looking accounting — slightly less exposed.

adjacent_careers.name: Forensic Accountant
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Investigation + judgment + court-admissible work — durable specialty.

adjacent_careers.name: Controller / CFO Track
adjacent_careers.reasoning: People management + strategy; AI accelerates your team rather than replaces the role.

action_plan.title: Aim for the CPA, not a generic accounting degree
action_plan.description: The licensure is the moat. Programs aligned with the 150-credit CPA requirement are the durable path.

action_plan.title: Get good with one ERP (NetSuite, Workday, or SAP)
action_plan.description: Domain + tooling expertise beats generic accounting skills in a more-automated world.

action_plan.title: Take a financial analysis or data analytics elective
action_plan.description: The advisory and analyst pivots reward people who can model, not just record.

action_plan.title: Intern at a small business, not just Big Four
action_plan.description: Small-business exposure shows you the full books and the relationship work that survives automation.

follow_up_suggestions.label: Is the CPA still worth it?
